terms.definition: Pouring water on plants so they can grow healthy and strong.
terms.exampleUsage: Watering the plants every morning helps them grow well.

terms.definition: Words that have the same ending sound, like 'tree' and 'free'. Poems often use rhymes to sound musical.
terms.exampleUsage: The poem has a nice rhyme - 'sun' rhymes with 'done'.

terms.definition: Thinking of others before yourself. Doing things to help others without expecting anything in return.
terms.exampleUsage: The giving tree was selfless - it gave everything to make the boy happy.

terms.definition: A cool area sheltered from the hot sun, often under a tree or roof.
terms.exampleUsage: The boy rested in the shade of the big tree on a hot day.

terms.definition: The short part of a tree trunk that remains after the tree is cut down.
terms.exampleUsage: After the tree was cut, only the stump was left for the old man to sit on.

terms.definition: The form of a verb that shows when an action takes place - in the past, present, or future.
Feeling thankful for something someone has done for you or given you.
terms.exampleUsage: We should be grateful to trees for giving us fruits, shade, and fresh air.

terms.definition: The thick main stem of a tree from which branches grow. Also, the tree gave its trunk to the boy to make a boat.
terms.exampleUsage: The boy used the trunk of the tree to build a boat and sail away.

terms.definition: When a flower opens up and shows its petals. Also means to grow and develop well.
terms.exampleUsage: If you water the plants properly, they will bloom with beautiful flowers.

terms.definition: Ready to give and share with others happily, without being asked or expecting anything back.
terms.exampleUsage: The tree was generous - it happily gave its apples, branches, and trunk to the boy.
